2605.20267.title: Generation of Heterogeneous PET Images from Uniform Organ Activity Maps Using a Pretrained Domain-Adapted Diffusion Model
2605.20267.paper_summary: Synthetic PET images are valuable for quantitative imaging workflow development, scalable virtual imaging trials, and deep learning model training, but conventional physics-based simulation approaches are computationally intensive, limited in anatomical variability, and often fail to capture heterogeneous PET uptake. This study developed a pretrained domain-adapted diffusion (PAD) model for anatomy-conditioned PET synthesis from uniform organ activity maps. PAD adopts a natural-image pretrained text-to-image decoder with an upstream conditioning encoder and a downstream PET-domain adapter. A two-phase training strategy was used, with the first phase learning coarse uptake distributions and the second refining local image details. Uniform organ activity maps were generated from CT-based segmentations by assigning each organ its mean uptake from the paired PET image. Evaluation included quantitative accuracy, noise assessment, radiomic analysis, tumor segmentation performance, and a human observer study. PAD-generated images achieved high quantitative accuracy, with concordance correlation coefficients above 0.92 between organ mean SUVs and assigned activity values. The synthesized images showed noise levels and texture characteristics similar to target PET images and produced comparable tumor segmentation performance. In a two-alternative forced-choice observer study, four readers achieved approximately 50% accuracy, indicating visual indistinguishability between synthesized and target images. PAD also generated realistic PET images from XCAT-derived activity maps, demonstrating compatibility with phantom-based anatomical priors. Overall, PAD provides a diffusion-based framework for generating clinically relevant heterogeneous PET images from uniform organ activity maps derived from clinical segmentations or digital phantoms, supporting data augmentation and downstream imaging studies.
2605.20267.paper_summary_zh: 合成的PET影像對於定量影像工作流程的開發、可擴展的虛擬影像試驗以及深度學習模型訓練具有重要價值，但傳統的基於物理的模擬方法計算量大，解剖變異性有限，並且常常無法捕捉異質性的PET攝取。這項研究開發了一種預訓練的領域適應擴散（PAD）模型，用於從均勻的器官活動圖合成解剖條件的PET影像。PAD採用了自然影像預訓練的文本到影像解碼器，並配備上游的條件編碼器和下游的PET領域適配器。使用了兩階段的訓練策略，第一階段學習粗略的攝取分佈，第二階段則精煉局部影像細節。均勻的器官活動圖是從基於CT的分割生成的，通過為每個器官分配其來自配對PET影像的平均攝取量來實現。評估包括定量準確性、噪聲評估、放射組學分析、腫瘤分割性能以及人類觀察者研究。PAD生成的影像達到了高定量準確性，器官平均SUV與分配活動值之間的協調相關係數超過0.92。合成的影像顯示出與目標PET影像相似的噪聲水平和質地特徵，並產生了可比擬的腫瘤分割性能。在一項兩選一的強迫選擇觀察者研究中，四位讀者的準確率約為50%，表明合成影像與目標影像在視覺上無法區分。PAD還從XCAT衍生的活動圖生成了現實的PET影像，顯示出與基於幻影的解剖先驗相容。總體而言，PAD提供了一個基於擴散的框架，用於從臨床分割或數位幻影衍生的均勻器官活動圖生成臨床相關的異質性PET影像，支持數據增強和下游影像研究。
2605.20267.author: Suya Li et.al.
2605.20267.authors: Suya Li, Kaushik Dutta, Debojyoti Pal, Jingqin Luo, Kooresh I. Shoghi
